regime_justification: USDJPY at 159.01, 0.18 yen below MOF watch line; JGB 10Y at 2.78%, approaching 29-year highs; BOJ June hike (80% priced) creates intervention/policy tension; vol subdued despite fragile setup.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY hovering 18 pips below 160 psychological threshold that triggered prior MOF interventions; JGB yields at multi-decade highs; Finance Minister Katayama pledged 'bold action as needed'; BOJ June hike priced at 80%.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: USDJPY 18 pips from 160; JGBs spiking; MOF rhetoric active; June BOJ meeting June 15-16 inside horizon.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: BOJ meeting (June 15-16) with 80% chance of hike to 1.0%; Bessent backing Ueda; spread at 187 bps but vol compressed—setup fragile.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Post-BOJ adjustment window; if hike lands, spread compression begins but yen may not follow if US yields keep rising; Hormuz risk persists.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Initial BOJ hike response likely absorbed; unless Hormuz shock intensifies or Fed pivots hawkish, carry can stabilize at new equilibrium by early June.

bottom_line: Stage 2: Pre-Intervention. USDJPY 18 pips below 160 watch line; JGB 10Y at 2.78% (29-yr high); BOJ June hike 80% priced; Finance Minister Katayama pledged intervention readiness. Spread wide (187 bps) but vol suppressed—setup brittle. One-week grade C (watchful); if BOJ delivers and MOF acts, forced unwind trigger is cocked. Crypto (BTC $76.5k) correlating with risk-off tape.

Near Sept 1996 high (2.80%); strong GDP data + Hormuz inflation fears fueling rate-hike expectations

6-week high; USD strength on Fed hike speculation and safe-haven flows from Hormuz

trigger_map.note: Katayama: 'bold action as needed' post-G7; USDJPY 18 pips from 160; rate-check rumors circulating; catalyst = break above 160 or acceleration.
June 15-16 meeting: 80% chance of hike to 1.0%; Bessent backing Ueda; Takaichi dovish but may consent if framed as yen defense—watch for 25bp+ surprise.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: At 2.78%, 2 bps shy of 2.80% (Sept 1996 high); bear steepening accelerating—breakout would signal fiscal/inflation panic.
Currently 85; subdued despite JGB volatility—if MOVE crosses 100, signals rates-vol regime shift and forced repositioning.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl = 16,620 (WTI 104.52 × 159.01); Hormuz closed; Trump paused strike but no deal—further escalation = import cost spike, BOJ forced to hike faster.
No current data on 3M FX swap basis; if dollar funding tightens (basis widens sharply negative), yen carry unwind accelerates—monitor post-BOJ.

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY opened 159.07, drifted to 159.01 (−0.02%), sitting 18 pips below the 160 level that triggered MOF intervention in late April/early May. Finance Minister Katayama, speaking in Paris post-G7, reiterated Japan's readiness for 'bold action as needed' and confirmed G7 understanding of Japan's FX stance. JGB 10Y yield touched 2.80% intraday (29-year high) before pulling back to 2.78% as strong Q1 GDP data (0.5% q/q vs. 0.4% est) and BOJ board member Masu's hawkish remarks reinforced June hike expectations. US 10Y yield at 4.64% (16-month high) as Hormuz closure and stalled Iran talks fuel Fed hawkish repricing. Nikkei fell 1.23% on rising yields. BTC steady at $76.5k. No stage change—remain Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention), but MOF rhetoric and USDJPY proximity to 160 elevate intervention risk to 'cocked.'
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread = 186 bps (4.64% − 2.78%), down 1 bp but still historically wide. Carry-to-vol proxy unavailable (1M USDJPY implied vol data sparse; last available reading ~7.6% in late April per one source, suggesting compressed vol despite fragile setup). USDJPY 5-day change = +0.13%—pace is orderly, not panicked, which is why MOF has not yet intervened. ¥/bbl crude = 16,620 (104.52 × 159.01), extreme import cost pressure. USDJPY at 159.01 vs. 60-day mean ~157-158 (estimated from recent range)—modestly stretched but not extreme. We are in Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention): USDJPY at watch line, MOF vocal, BOJ June meeting (15-16) with 80% market pricing for hike to 1.0%, JGBs bear-steepening. Grades 1w/2w = C (watchful): BOJ meeting inside 2-week window is the dominant catalyst; if hike lands and MOF jawbones or intervenes, spread compression + vol spike = early unwind. Grade 3w = C (post-BOJ adjustment); 4w = D (unwind risk recedes if hike absorbed cleanly). Applied unwind prior on 1w/2w grades: data is genuinely ambiguous (vol subdued, spread wide, USDJPY drifting but not breaking)—absent the prior, might grade 1w D, but proximity to 160 + BOJ catalyst + MOF rhetoric justify C.
narrative.p3: Crypto liquidity: BTC at $76,489 (+0.21% 1d), holding the $76-77k zone after April's washout from $126k ATH. Open interest direction unavailable but prior correction flushed leverage. BTC correlating with risk-off: rose modestly as equities sold off and DXY strengthened—acting as safe-haven vs. risk assets but not vs. dollar. XRP data unavailable. BTC-DXY 30d correlation likely positive (both rising on Fed hawkish repricing). Implication: if yen carry unwinds (Stage 3-4), expect BTC to sell off alongside equities as liquidity drains and margin calls cascade. Current positioning: BTC is cleaner post-washout, but a forced unwind would test $70k support; break below = $60k in play. For the operator: BTC is not insulated from carry unwind—treat as risk-on proxy, size accordingly.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: One-week grade C, two-week C. BOJ meeting June 15-16 (inside 2w horizon) is the pivot. Markets price 80% chance of hike to 1.0%; Bessent's backing of Ueda and Katayama's intervention rhetoric create policy/FX tension. If BOJ delivers 25bp and signals more to come, spread compresses fast but USDJPY may not follow if US yields stay elevated—MOF may intervene to jawbone or stabilize. If BOJ holds or hikes only 15bp, yen selloff resumes and intervention risk spikes. Data ambiguous (vol low, spread wide), applied unwind prior to break tie toward C.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Two-week C, three-week C. Post-BOJ adjustment window. If hike lands, initial spread compression (US 10Y stable, JGB rising) but carry remains positive at 1.5%+ if US yields hold 4.6%. Yen strength depends on BOJ forward guidance and MOF action. Hormuz risk persists (no Iran deal)—further oil spike could force accelerated BOJ tightening or trigger risk-off unwind. Three-week scenario: BOJ hikes, spread narrows to ~150 bps, USDJPY drifts to 155-157, equities volatile but no forced unwind unless exogenous shock (Iran escalation, Fed surprise hike signal).
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Three-week C, four-week D. By early June (4w out), if BOJ hike is absorbed and Hormuz situation stabilizes (unlikely but possible), carry recalibrates at lower spread but positive. USDJPY may settle 155-157 range, vol declines, intervention risk fades. Grade D reflects scenario where initial volatility passes and no second-order shock emerges. Non-monotonic grading valid: risk peaks around BOJ meeting (2-3w), then recedes if catalyst resolves cleanly.

watch_list: USDJPY break above 160.00 (triggers MOF intervention, possibly forced unwind if aggressive)
BOJ June 15-16 decision: size of hike (15bp, 25bp, or hold), forward guidance, bond taper plan—any surprise accelerates timeline
JGB 10Y yield break above 2.80% (Sept 1996 high)—signals fiscal panic, forces BOJ hand, widens MOVE
Hormuz / Iran developments: deal = oil collapses, unwind risk falls; escalation = oil spikes, inflation shock, forced BOJ tightening
USDJPY 1M implied vol: if spikes above 10-12%, signals positioning shift and unwind preparation
Fed speakers / data (CPI, jobs): any signal of June/July hike keeps US yields elevated, delays spread compression
